---
title: "HW-1(Markov)"
author: "Emery Berry"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

```{r}
# Problem 1 part b
n <- 100
p <- 0.02

P_exact <- pbinom(1, size = n, prob = p)
P_exact
```

```{r}
#Problem 3 part b

set.seed(1)

# function from part a
f <- function(u){
  u^4 / (1+u^6)
}

# Create the list of sample sizes
x_vals <- seq(1, 5, by = 0.1)
N_vals <- floor(10^x_vals)


# Compute non-Monte Carlo Integration

I_true <- integrate(function(x) 1 / (1 + x^6), lower = 1, upper = Inf)$value

#Prepare an empty list for E(N)

E_vals <- numeric(length(N_vals))

#For each N:
for (i in seq_along(N_vals)) {
  N <- N_vals[i]

  #Sample points uniformly in [0,1]^2
  U <- runif(N)  # x-coordinates
  V <- runif(N)  # y-coordinates

  #Determine which points are below the curve y = f(u)
  below_curve <- (V <= f(U))

  #Fraction below curve is the Monte Carlo estimate E(N)
  E_vals[i] <- mean(below_curve)
}


#Plot E(N) vs N with a logarithmic horizontal axis

plot(N_vals, E_vals,
     log = "x",         
     type = "b",         
     xlab = "N (log scale)",
     ylab = "E(N) = fraction of points below f",
     main = "Monte Carlo estimate of I by area under f")


#Add horizontal reference line at the non-MC integral value

abline(h = I_true, lty = 2)

legend("bottomright",
       legend = c("E(N) (Monte Carlo)", "Reference (numerical integration)"),
       lty = c(1, 2),
       pch = c(1, NA),
       bty = "n")


```

```{r}
# Problem 4 part b

set.seed(1)

N <- 1e5

# Simulate arrival times (hours after 6PM)
A <- runif(N)  # Agnes
M <- runif(N)  # Moe
D <- runif(N)  # Dorothy

# Time when all three have arrived
T <- pmax(A, M, D)

# Plot histogram normalized as a PDF
hist(T,
     breaks = 50,
     freq = FALSE,           
     xlim = c(0, 1),
     ylim = c(0, 3),
     xlab = "T (hours after 6PM)",
     main = "Jill waiting time: simulation vs theoretical PDF")

# Overlay theoretical PDF: f_T(t) = 3 t^2 on [0,1]
t_grid <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 500)
lines(t_grid, 3 * t_grid^2, lwd = 2)

legend("topleft",
       legend = c("Simulated histogram (density)", "Theoretical PDF: 3 t^2"),
       lty = c(NA, 1),
       lwd = c(NA, 2),
       pch = c(15, NA),
       bty = "n")
```
